drama.descriptionFocus: domestic space, institutional architecture, and the objects that carry unspoken history

dialogueSubtext.setup: what is not being said matters more than what is
dialogueSubtext.revelation: every answer contains a question the speaker avoids
dialogueSubtext.culmination: silence between lines carries the moral weight

monologueTriggers.setup: after a family ritual that exposes the fracture
monologueTriggers.revelation: when someone names what everyone avoided
monologueTriggers.culmination: after the truth arrives too late to prevent damage

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: protagonist
dialogueTurnBlueprints.intent: test whether the other person remembers the same event
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: I need to know if I am alone in this reading
dialogueTurnBlueprints.lineHint: indirect question about a shared moment

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: counterpart
dialogueTurnBlueprints.intent: protect the family version without lying outright
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: I know more than I can say right now
dialogueTurnBlueprints.lineHint: careful rephrasing of the shared narrative
dialogueTurnBlueprints.reactionBeat: protagonist notices the gap between versions

detective-police.descriptionFocus: procedural spaces, evidence rooms, and the architecture of institutional power

dialogueSubtext.setup: every official answer contains an unspoken boundary
dialogueSubtext.investigation: the witness knows more than the transcript shows
dialogueSubtext.culmination: accusation and defense share the same factual ground

monologueTriggers.setup: after reviewing a file that does not match the official timeline
monologueTriggers.investigation: when a protected name surfaces in an unprotected context
monologueTriggers.culmination: after the evidence chain points upward instead of outward

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: protagonist
dialogueTurnBlueprints.intent: pressure the witness without breaking procedure
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: I know you are holding back, and I need you to decide

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: counterpart
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: I will help, but not at the cost of my position
dialogueTurnBlueprints.reactionBeat: protagonist files the omission for later

science-fiction.descriptionFocus: engineered environments, interface surfaces, and the visible signs of system control

dialogueSubtext.investigation: every system response carries a political choice
dialogueSubtext.culmination: consent and control occupy the same sentence

monologueTriggers.setup: after a memory trace reveals the gap between maintenance and control
monologueTriggers.investigation: when the protagonist realizes the anomaly has a political author
monologueTriggers.culmination: after choosing between engineered peace and contested freedom

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: protagonist
dialogueTurnBlueprints.intent: expose the political intention behind a technical decision
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: I know this is not maintenance, and you know I know

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: counterpart
dialogueTurnBlueprints.intent: defend the system without denying the human cost
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: stability requires choices no one wants to name
dialogueTurnBlueprints.lineHint: pragmatic argument that avoids moral language

fantasy.descriptionFocus: ritual spaces, ancestral architecture, and the visible signs of magical cost

dialogueSubtext.setup: ritual language conceals the founding crime
dialogueSubtext.investigation: every oath carries a price the speaker does not name
dialogueSubtext.culmination: truthful speech burns through inherited protection

monologueTriggers.setup: after the valley answers with omen before speech
monologueTriggers.investigation: when the protagonist reads the founding vow correctly for the first time
monologueTriggers.culmination: after choosing between inherited shelter and truthful sacrifice

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: protagonist
dialogueTurnBlueprints.intent: name the binding oath without submitting to it
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: I will not repeat the lie, but I need to understand it
dialogueTurnBlueprints.lineHint: ritual question that challenges the ritual answer
dialogueTurnBlueprints.reactionBeat: authority figure responds with enforced tradition

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: counterpart
dialogueTurnBlueprints.intent: guide without revealing the full cost
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: I know what truth will cost you, and I am not sure you should pay it
dialogueTurnBlueprints.lineHint: cautious warning framed as practical advice
dialogueTurnBlueprints.reactionBeat: protagonist recognizes the protection beneath the caution

romance-relational.descriptionFocus: shared workspaces, domestic objects, and the spaces where routine becomes intimate

dialogueSubtext.setup: practical language carries emotional freight neither character names
dialogueSubtext.reversal: what starts as collaboration becomes confession
dialogueSubtext.culmination: honesty removes the last professional shield

monologueTriggers.setup: after a practical gesture reveals more than either character intended
monologueTriggers.reversal: when the balance between professional control and vulnerable attachment flips
monologueTriggers.culmination: after choosing interdependence over guarded distance

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: protagonist
dialogueTurnBlueprints.intent: test whether honesty can coexist with collaboration
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: I need to know if you feel this too, without saying it first

dialogueTurnBlueprints.speaker: counterpart
dialogueTurnBlueprints.intent: maintain professional distance while feeling the shift
dialogueTurnBlueprints.subtext: I want to name this, but naming it changes everything
dialogueTurnBlueprints.lineHint: careful response that almost crosses the line
